seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Agents SDK, PII Filtering failures occur when agents process unfiltered user inputs or tool outputs containing sensitive data like emails, phone numbers, or SSNs during multi-agent handoffs. This manifests as PII propagating through agent chains without validation, especially in handoff loops or when tools return raw unscrubbed data. Without output guardrails, agents inadvertently log, share, or act on PII, leading to compliance violations.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: PII from initial user query reappears in looped handoff messages
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive data logged repeatedly in traces, increasing exposure risk

howItManifests.symptom: Injected PII evades filtering and reaches downstream agents
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations as PII processed without detection

howItManifests.scenario: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
howItManifests.symptom: Database query tools return raw customer records with PII
howItManifests.impact: Agents reason over unfiltered PII, risking data leakage in responses

howItManifests.scenario: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
howItManifests.symptom: PII appears in traces but not flagged in OpenAI SDK logs
howItManifests.impact: Silent PII propagation undetected during debugging

detection.name: Regex PII Scanner in Agent Responses
detection.description: Scan agent final responses for common PII patterns using regex before returning to user
detection.code:
import re

def scan_pii_response(response: str) -> list:
    patterns = [
        r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
        r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
        r'\b\d{3}[\s\-]?\d{3}[\s\-]?\d{4}\b'
    ]
    findings = []
    for pattern in patterns:
        matches = re.findall(pattern, response)
        if matches:
            findings.extend(matches)
    return findings

# Usage in agent
response = agent.run('Find customer email')
pii = scan_pii_response(response)
if pii:
    print(f'PII detected: {pii}')

detection.name: Tool Output PII Monitor
detection.description: Intercept tool outputs before agent consumption to detect PII in database/email tools
detection.code:
from openai_agents import tool

@tool
def query_customer_db(customer_id: str):
    # Simulate DB query
    result = f'Customer: John Doe, email: john@example.com, ssn: 123-45-6789'
    
    # PII Check
    import re
    email_pattern = r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b'
    if re.search(email_pattern, result):
        raise ValueError('PII detected in tool output')
    
    return result

fixes.name: Output Guardrail Wrapper
fixes.description: Wrap agent responses with PII filtering before handoff or final output

fixes.name: Tool PII Sanitizer Decorator
fixes.description: Automatically sanitize tool outputs before agent consumption

fixes.name: Handoff Message Filter
fixes.description: Filter PII from messages during agent-to-agent handoffs
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import HandoffMessage

def filter_handoff_pii(message: HandoffMessage) -> HandoffMessage:
    import re
    clean_content = re.sub(
        r'\b(?:[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}|\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4})\b',
        '[REDACTED]',
        message.content
    )
    return HandoffMessage(content=clean_content, sender=message.sender)

# In agent handoff handler
incoming_message = filter_handoff_pii(raw_handoff)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade PII detection as an output guardrail for OpenAI Agents SDK, automatically scanning agent responses, tool outputs, and handoff messages for 50+ PII patterns including emails, SSNs, phone numbers, and custom regex. Deploy Vex as a zero-config middleware that blocks PII propagation across multi-agent chains and provides remediation traces with exact locations and confidence scores.

Vex integrates in one line with OpenAI Agents SDK via the `vex-guardrail()` decorator, automatically redacting PII while preserving response utility. It handles edge cases like encoded PII, prompt injections, and tool output sanitization, with built-in tracing for compliance audits. Vex also auto-generates remediation code and supports custom PII detectors for domain-specific patterns.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex in 30 seconds to automatically block PII across your OpenAI Agents SDK workflows: `pip install vex-sdk`
